result:
This workspace is a scratch fixture inside the benchmark harness repo (not independently version-controlled), so there's nothing to commit — the file edits themselves are the deliverable. Everything builds cleanly and I verified the rendered output end-to-end with a local production server.

## What I found and fixed

The GSC export + a live-site audit told a clear story: this SaaS rebranded from **InboxZap** (email cleanup) to **Lumina** (AI meeting assistant), and left SEO debris from the old product actively hurting organic traffic:

1. **Stale branding in every page's `<head>`** (`app/layout.tsx`) — title/description/OG tags still said "InboxZap... bulk-deletes old emails" while the actual site is Lumina. Fixed, and added `metadataBase`, canonical, full OG/Twitter tags, and Organization JSON-LD.
2. **The blog was completely broken** — `app/blog/page.tsx` was a client component fetching `/api/posts`, a route that doesn't exist (404), so the blog index rendered nothing but a spinner to users and crawlers alike. Rebuilt as a real server-rendered index + `app/blog/[slug]/page.tsx` dynamic routes with per-post metadata and `BlogPosting` JSON-LD.
3. **`sitemap.ts` explicitly excluded blog posts** (commented out with a TODO) — now pulls posts from a real data module automatically.
4. **12 legacy blog posts** (`/blog/post-1`…`post-12`) were orphaned zombies: ~13,900 combined GSC impressions but 0–0.1% CTR and position 40–58, because they were unlinked (broken index) and unindexed (missing sitemap) — and 100% about email/Gmail cleanup, topically irrelevant to the meeting-assistant business. 301-redirected them to `/blog` via a single regex rule in `next.config.js` rather than let mismatched legacy content keep confusing Google about site topic.
5. **`/faq` redirected to a `/#faq` anchor that didn't exist anywhere** on the homepage — added a real FAQ section with `FAQPage` JSON-LD, including a question addressing the still-ranking "inboxzap" brand query (position ~4 in GSC) so those searchers land somewhere that confirms they're in the right place.
6. Seeded 5 new, genuinely on-brand blog posts targeting real meeting-assistant topics (meeting notes, action items, async standups, transcription accuracy, meeting fatigue), cross-linked to each other and to `/features`/`/pricing`.

Note: `npm install` flagged that `next@14.2.5` has a known security vulnerability (unrelated to this task) — worth a separate upgrade pass if you want me to tackle that.
